title: Particles & Special Vocabulary Mastery
description: Master Japanese particles and special vocabulary with tricks, examples, and interactive learning

sections.name: Topic Particle
sections.mainFunction: Marks the topic of the sentence
sections.trick: Think of は as pointing a spotlight 🔦 on the topic - 'As for THIS...'
usageTips: Used for general statements and known information
Can show contrast (especially with emphasis)
Cannot be used with question words (なに、だれ, etc.)

examples.explanation: は marks '私' as the topic
は marks 'today' as what we're talking about

examples.japanese: リンゴは好きですが、バナナは嫌いです。
examples.romaji: Ringo wa suki desu ga, banana wa kirai desu.
examples.english: I like apples, but I hate bananas.
examples.explanation: は shows contrast between two items

commonMistakes.explanation: Never use は with question words! Use を, が, or other particles instead.

vsComparison.difference: は = topic/contrast | が = subject/new info
vsComparison.example: 私は犬が好きです。(As for me, I like dogs) - は marks topic, が marks what is liked

quiz.question: Fill in: 私___学生です。
quiz.explanation: Use は to mark yourself as the topic

quiz.question: Which is correct?
quiz.explanation: Never use は with question words!

sections.name: Subject Particle
sections.mainFunction: Marks the grammatical subject, introduces new information
sections.trick: が highlights NEW or IMPORTANT info - like a ✨ spotlight on something new!
usageTips: Use with question words (who, what, which)
Introduces new information or emphasis
Required with certain verbs (好き、嫌い、分かる、できる、ある、いる)
Used in relative clauses

examples.explanation: が must be used with question word 誰

examples.japanese: 私は日本語が分かります。
examples.explanation: 分かる requires が for what is understood

commonMistakes.wrong: 私は日本語は分かります。
commonMistakes.right: 私は日本語が分かります。
commonMistakes.explanation: Verbs like 分かる, 好き, できる require が for the object

quiz.question: 私___寿司___好きです。
quiz.explanation: は for topic (I), が for what is liked (sushi)

quiz.question: Which needs が?
quiz.explanation: 好き is a special adjective requiring が

sections.name: Object Particle
sections.mainFunction: Marks the direct object of an action
sections.trick: を points like an arrow ➡️ from action to target!
usageTips: Use with action verbs (eat, drink, read, watch, etc.)
Also marks the space through which you move (歩く、走る、泳ぐ)
Pronounced 'o' not 'wo' in modern Japanese

examples.explanation: を marks 'book' as the direct object
を marks the space you move through

sections.name: Direction/Time/Existence Particle
sections.mainFunction: Marks destination, time, location of existence, indirect object
sections.trick: に is like a GPS pin 📍 - exact point in time or space!
usageTips: Destination of movement (行く、来る、帰る + に)
Specific time points (3時に、月曜日に)
Location where things exist (います、あります + に)
Indirect object (person you give to, etc.)
Purpose (買い物に行く = go TO shop)

examples.japanese: 机の上に本があります。
examples.romaji: Tsukue no ue ni hon ga arimasu.
examples.english: There is a book on the desk.

examples.japanese: 友達にプレゼントをあげます。
examples.english: I give a present to my friend.

vsComparison.difference: に = destination/static location | で = action location
vsComparison.example: 学校に行きます (go TO school) vs 学校で勉強します (study AT school)

quiz.question: 東京___住んでいます。
quiz.explanation: 住む (to live) uses に for location

sections.name: Location/Means Particle
sections.mainFunction: Marks location of action or means/method
sections.trick: で = where the ACTION happens! Think: 'doing it WITH/AT'
usageTips: Location where action occurs
Means/method (by car, with chopsticks, in Japanese)
Material/ingredient (made of, made with)
Reason/cause

examples.explanation: で marks where the action (studying) happens

sections.name: Also/Too Particle
sections.trick: も = ME TOO! ✋ Adds something to the list
usageTips: Replaces は, が, を (but not に, で, etc.)
Can combine with other particles: にも, でも, からも
Use with negatives for 'not...either'

examples.japanese: 日本にも行きたいです。
examples.english: I want to go to Japan too.

quiz.question: A: 私は学生です。 B: 私___学生です。

sections.name: Possessive/Modifier Particle
sections.trick: の = 's in English! Links things together like a chain 🔗
usageTips: Possession (my, your, his, etc.)
Connects two nouns (modifies)
Can replace nouns (this one = これの → この)
Shows origin or category

examples.english: Please give me the red one.

sections.name: Question Particle
sections.trick: か = ? in English! Turns statements into questions
usageTips: Place at end of sentence for yes/no questions
With question words for WH-questions
Between options for 'or' (AかB)
With question word + か = some/any (何か、誰か、どこか)

examples.japanese: コーヒーか紅茶をください。
examples.english: Please give me coffee or tea.

quiz.question: How do you say 'somewhere' in Japanese?

specialVocabulary.title: Giving & Receiving Verbs
specialVocabulary.description: Master the tricky system of giving and receiving in Japanese
specialVocabulary.trick: Direction matters! あげる = ⬆️ up/away, くれる = ⬇️ down/to me, もらう = ⬅️ receive

items.example: 私は友達に本をあげました。
items.translation: I gave a book to my friend.

items.example: 友達が私に本をくれました。
items.translation: My friend gave me a book.

items.example: 私は友達に本をもらいました。
items.translation: I received a book from my friend.

quiz.question: I gave my teacher a present. → 先生___プレゼント___あげました。
quiz.explanation: に for recipient, を for object

quiz.question: My friend gave me a present. Which verb?
quiz.explanation: くれる when someone gives TO ME

specialVocabulary.title: Question Words
specialVocabulary.description: Essential question words to ask anything

items.tip: なに before particles, なん before です/の/counter
Always use が, never は!
どこか = somewhere, どこも = everywhere

items.example: なぜ日本語を勉強しますか？

items.translation: What do you think? (How do you think?)
items.tip: どう = how (in general), どのように = in what way
どれ = which one, どの = which + noun

comparison.example: 誰が来ますか？ → 田中さんが来ます。

comparison.reason: These words require が for object

funChallenges.title: Particle Master Challenge
funChallenges.description: Fill in ALL the particles correctly!
funChallenges.sentence: 私___毎朝7時___起きて、朝ご飯___食べて、学校___行きます。学校___友達___勉強します。
funChallenges.explanation: は(topic), に(time), を(object), に(destination), で(location), と(together with)

funChallenges.title: Speed Round
funChallenges.description: Quick! Choose the right particle in 5 seconds!
